claim: The best Claude today is Fable (current top model, offered across Chat/Cowork/Code).
check: Anthropic models page / claude.com — is Fable the current flagship name?

claim: Chat shape: keeps a 2-paragraph summary of who you are, connects to apps like Drive and Asana, has tools for making quick websites.
check: claude.ai feature docs: memory, connectors list (Asana included?), artifacts.

claim: Cowork spawns many Claude clones on command ('Claude, use subagents!').
check: Cowork docs/support: does it support subagents on user request?

claim: Cowork has its own restricted, ephemeral computer (VM reset between tasks?).
check: support.claude.com Cowork architecture article — is the VM ephemeral or persistent?

claim: Code has infinitely customizable extensions that anyone on the internet can create and share.
check: Claude Code docs: skills/MCP/plugins — creation and sharing story.

claim: Tag exists: Claude hired into your Slack channels as a teammate (@Claude).
check: Anthropic announcement / product page for Claude in Slack ('Tag') — name and scope.

claim: Front door links the appendix as ../appendix-tag.md, but appendix-tag.md is in the same directory as front-door.md — relative path broken as drafted.
check: Resolve link against the final published directory structure.

claim: Cowork Claudes are sealed off in a VM; the worst they can do is make a mess inside their padded room; a slip can't reach the rest of your machine.
check: Cowork security/architecture docs — sandbox boundaries, known reach-outs (host browser automation, connectors).

claim: You'll be delegating real work within fifteen minutes of installing; fifteen minutes after install the folder is mapped with a plan waiting.
check: Time a real first run: download+VM setup+inventory task; is 15 min honest?

claim: Claude Code is what the Anthropic engineers use for their own jobs / the thing they put the most effort into.
check: Anthropic blog posts/interviews on internal Claude Code usage.

claim: Free opens neither Cowork nor Code; Pro unlocks both; 'Cowork needs a paid plan — Pro or up.'
check: claude.com/pricing + support article on Cowork availability — note Team Standard exclusion risk.

claim: Pro costs ~$20/month.
check: claude.com/pricing current Pro price (monthly vs annual).

claim: https://claude.com/pricing resolves and is the plans/pricing page.
check: Fetch the URL; confirm no redirect to a different page.

claim: https://claude.com/download is where you download the Claude desktop app.
check: Fetch the URL; confirm it serves the desktop app for Mac and Windows.

claim: Cowork is opened by clicking 'Cowork' in the desktop app's left sidebar.
check: Current desktop app UI / support screenshots — tab name and location.

claim: Windows fix: enable 'Virtual Machine Platform' via 'Turn Windows features on or off', check the box, restart.
check: support.claude.com Cowork Windows requirements article — exact feature name and steps.

claim: Cowork's first run downloads about 2 GB (the virtual machine).
check: Official docs or fresh install measurement; research marks 2 GB as medium confidence.

claim: The Chat tab works before Cowork is set up, so you can paste install errors there for help.
check: Desktop app: is chat usable pre-Cowork-setup on a paid account?

claim: The folder you pick is the whole workspace — what's inside is all Claude can see and touch, and nothing else is.
check: Cowork docs: folder scoping vs connectors/browser reach; can it request more?

claim: Cowork sometimes saves/hides files inside its virtual machine instead of your folder; you must say where to put outputs.
check: Cowork docs/community reports — where outputs land by default; still a real failure mode?

claim: Screenshots can be pasted straight into the chat input.
check: Desktop app: image paste into chat and Code input bars.

claim: Dictation: click/tap the microphone icon in the chat input bar.
check: Desktop app UI — mic icon present in Cowork and Code input bars.

claim: Dictation shows your words landing as text while you speak.
check: Desktop app dictation: live transcription vs transcribe-on-stop.

claim: There is a model picker by the input bar; pick the best Claude offered.
check: Desktop app Cowork UI — model picker location and options.

claim: Wispr Flow (https://wisprflow.ai/) is the upgrade for dictating everywhere on your computer.
check: Fetch wisprflow.ai — product alive, system-wide dictation, right URL.

claim: Claude overclaims completion: says 'done, it all worked' when it quietly skipped rows / means 'attempted'.
check: Still a documented failure mode for current models (Anthropic docs/evals, community).

claim: Cowork can open/display a file to the user on command ('open the file at the end', 'open the table next to the first PDF').
check: Can Cowork open files on the user's screen across the VM boundary, or only present them in-app?

claim: A file saved in your folder stays where you and every future session can find it.
check: Cowork docs: picked-folder writes persist to host across sessions.

claim: Asking for a running markdown log file, updated as it works, is a reliable pattern.
check: Sanity-test in Cowork: does the agent maintain the log through a task?

claim: Agent outputs can run book-length; you must specify a skimmable shape or receive the book.
check: Current default verbosity of agent reports — is the warning still earned?

claim: Cowork runs tasks side by side: start one, open a new task, start the next.
check: Cowork docs/UI: concurrent tasks supported; what the 'new task' control is called.

claim: Each Cowork task gets its own Claude with its own attention span; tasks don't share notes and don't wait for each other.
check: Cowork architecture: per-task isolation (context, VM, filesystem).

claim: Cowork has a mode that stops asking about routine actions; you can turn it on.
check: Cowork settings: name and location of the auto-approve/trust mode.

claim: Deletion still waits for your yes even with the auto-approve mode on.
check: Cowork permission docs: are deletions always confirmed under the relaxed mode?

claim: Prompt injection is well documented in the research literature but so far rare in practice, not worth organizing your life around.
check: Recent incident reports / Anthropic security guidance — still rare in the wild as of publish date?

claim: Cowork tasks stop when the laptop closes; it can't keep working after you close the lid.
check: Cowork docs: local VM lifecycle on sleep; any cloud continuation?

claim: You can queue a Cowork task from your phone while others run.
check: Claude mobile app: visibility/creation of Cowork tasks.

claim: Standing instructions live at Settings > Cowork and every session reads them whatever folder it's in.
check: Desktop app settings — exact path and behavior of Cowork instructions field.

claim: A context file in the folder is read by Claude at the start of every Cowork session there.
check: Does Cowork auto-read a project context file (CLAUDE.md-equivalent)? Which filename?

claim: Cowork skills: applied when a task matches, or type '/' and pick by name.
check: Cowork skills docs — auto-trigger and slash-picker existence.

claim: Install someone else's skill in Cowork by uploading a ZIP or browsing the built-in directory.
check: Cowork skills docs — ZIP upload flow and in-app skill directory.

claim: Cowork's VM can't reach GitHub (filtered network), so install-from-GitHub steps from Code tutorials won't work there.
check: Cowork network egress policy — is github.com blocked from the VM?

claim: Skills are edited conversationally: 'add this to my report skill as a non-negotiable rule' updates it mid-conversation.
check: Can a Cowork session edit installed skills? Docs or live test.

claim: Cowork is centrally managed — Anthropic decides which services connect and which programs run, once for everyone.
check: Cowork docs: can users add MCP servers/connectors themselves, or is it fixed?

claim: Everything from the Cowork track transfers whole to Code — folders, dictation, trust protocol, running many, the reuse arc under different names.
check: Verify each equivalent exists in Code (dictation in Code tab, instructions file, skills, parallel sessions).

check: Resolve link against the final published structure.

claim: Title formats disagree: code-01 'K1 — Install what the engineers use' and code-06 'K6 — Power-ups' vs plain titles + 'Track, lesson N' subtitles elsewhere; cowork-05 calls code-01 'Code track, lesson 1'.
check: Pick one naming scheme; update titles and every cross-reference.

claim: Unresolved bracket placeholders remain: two '[alts: ...]' in front-door, '[what's the key combo on Windows?]' in code-01.
check: Grep 04-drafts for '[' placeholders; resolve before publish.

claim: Claude Code: every file you can open, it can open; every program you can run, it can run.

claim: Cmd+, opens settings in the desktop app on Mac; Windows combo unknown (bracket).
check: Desktop app: settings shortcut on Mac and Windows.

claim: Settings > Claude Code has a toggle named 'Allow auto permissions mode'.

claim: There is a 'Code' icon next to 'Chat' on the desktop app's left sidebar.

claim: Input-bar dropdowns offer: Local (vs Cloud or Default), a folder picker, and Auto mode.
check: Desktop app Code tab — dropdown names and options.

claim: Auto mode stops asking permission for routine actions and interrupts only for risky ones.
check: code.claude.com/docs permission-modes — auto mode behavior AND availability (research-preview gating).

claim: Terminal install: curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash (Mac); irm https://claude.ai/install.ps1 | iex (Windows PowerShell); then type claude and sign in via browser.
check: code.claude.com/docs quickstart — current install commands and script URLs.

claim: The desktop app's Code tab is the same engine as terminal Claude Code, just with buttons; nothing else differs.
check: Docs/release notes — feature parity between app Code tab and CLI (modes, checkpoints, skills).

claim: Sign-in loop fix: quit the app fully and reopen; if that fails, sign out of both browser and app and retry.
check: support.claude.com troubleshooting — recommended fix for auth loops.

claim: macOS asks per-folder access (Documents/Downloads); denying means Claude works blind.
check: macOS TCC behavior for the Claude app — dialog appears, denial blocks file reads.

claim: Windows guidance: where the guide writes Cmd, try Ctrl first.
check: Verify for the shortcuts actually used (settings, new session) on Windows.

claim: Claude Code deletes conversation history after 30 days by default; telling it 'keep all my conversation history' makes it change its own setting.
check: Claude Code settings docs (cleanupPeriodDays) — default value and whether Claude can edit its own settings file.

claim: Google Docs: File > Download gets you a text copy to drop in the folder.
check: Current Google Docs menu — path and available text formats.

claim: Anything sitting in the working folder, Claude Code can open and read, PDFs included.
check: Claude Code docs — native PDF/file reading support and limits.

claim: When a file format fights you, export as text — a CSV, not an XLSX (CSV works better for Claude Code).
check: Does current Claude Code handle XLSX natively (skills)? Is the advice stale?

claim: A plan file is just a document; open it in anything you'd open a document with.
check: Plans save as plain markdown/text in the folder by default.

claim: Claude can offer configurations under which it cannot delete Drive files.
check: Do such setups exist (read-only OAuth scopes, connector settings)? Name one concretely.

claim: Google Drive already keeps snapshots (version history) as a passive protection.
check: Google Drive version history + trash retention limits — does 'snapshots' overstate?

claim: 'Set up save-points here so everything can be undone, and save automatically as you go' — one plain-language request, takes about a minute, yields working git auto-commits.
check: Live test: does the naive phrasing reliably produce git init + ongoing commits in ~1 min?

claim: The machinery underneath save-points is git, and that's all you ever need to know about it.
check: Any first-run git friction (identity config prompts) that breaks 'all you need to know'?

claim: With save-points on, any change to your files rewinds — which is what makes auto mode reasonable to run.
check: Git limits: untracked files, ignored files, actions outside the folder, non-file actions (sends/deletes elsewhere).

claim: Claude Code keeps checkpoints of its own edits; you can ask it to rewind what it just did before setting anything up.
check: Claude Code checkpoints/rewind docs — exists in the surface readers use (app vs CLI), scope of what rewinds.

claim: Rate limits cap how much Claude your plan buys per stretch of hours; if parallel sessions hit them, the answer is the bigger plan, not the worse model.
check: support.claude.com usage-limit articles + Max tier existence/naming.

claim: Cmd/Ctrl + N opens a fresh session in the desktop app.
check: Desktop app keyboard shortcuts — new session binding on Mac and Windows.

claim: Telling Code 'do it with subagents' makes it hire helper Claudes and fold their work back together.
check: Claude Code subagents docs — natural-language trigger works without setup.

claim: There is a token counter at the bottom of a session.
check: Desktop app Code tab UI — visible context/token counter and its location.

claim: The token counter reads something like '300,000 of 1,000,000' (implies a 1M-token context window).
check: Current context window for the default model/plan in Claude Code — 1M for whom?

claim: Past about 200,000 tokens, do the handoff (summary file + prompt for a fresh agent).
check: Sane threshold relative to actual window (F075) and observed degradation point.

claim: Tokens are just a fancy term for words, or similarly-sized chunks of text.
check: ~0.75 words/token; is the simplification close enough for the counter math?

claim: The quality of long conversations degrades for structural, machine-learning reasons — nothing more tokens will fix.
check: Long-context degradation ('context rot') literature + applies to current models.
